symptoms.symptom_name: Concurrent Crohn's GI flare (colicky pain, watery diarrhoea)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Enteropathic arthritis secondary to Crohn's flare
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Joint inflammation temporally locked to a GI flare is the signature of enteropathic (peripheral) arthritis.
Active diarrhoea and flatus argue against complete mechanical obstruction.

symptoms.symptom_name: Warm fluctuant polyarticular large-joint effusions (both knees, right ankle)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Enteropathic arthritis secondary to Crohn's flare
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Type 1 peripheral enteropathic arthritis is pauci/polyarticular, asymmetric large-joint, and tracks IBD activity.
Warm effusions in an immunosuppressed patient could be septic; polyarticularity lowers but does not eliminate the risk.
Polyarticular large-joint distribution linked to IBD is atypical for gout.

symptoms.symptom_name: No single dramatically hotter/redder joint; no high spiking fever/rigors

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Polyarticular, similarly-affected joints without a single fiery joint or rigors are less typical of joint sepsis, but aspiration is still needed to exclude it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Soft non-rigid abdomen, passing diarrhoea/flatus, no faeculent vomiting

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Passage of stool/flatus and a soft abdomen argue strongly against mechanical obstruction.
Absence of board-like rigidity argues against peritonitis, though this must be re-examined.
Ongoing diarrhoea with dry mucosa and tachycardia indicates significant dehydration needing rehydration and monitoring.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Enteropathic arthritis secondary to Crohn's flare
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Loss of biologic cover precipitates disease flare across gut and joints.
Immunosuppression modestly raises septic-arthritis risk and lowers the threshold to exclude it.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Enteropathic arthritis secondary to Crohn's flare
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Low-grade temperature fits systemic inflammation; a high spiking fever would raise sepsis concern.

symptoms.symptom_name: Warm fluctuant effusions; hyperactive bowel sounds; diffuse LQ tenderness; no peritonism/cellulitis (examination)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Enteropathic arthritis secondary to Crohn's flare
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Active synovitis with an inflamed but non-peritonitic abdomen maps to an enteropathic flare.
Fluctuant effusions in an immunosuppressed patient require aspiration to exclude infection.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Enteropathic arthritis secondary to Crohn's flare
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: IBD is the substrate for enteropathic arthritis.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Enteropathic arthritis secondary to Crohn's flare
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: A simultaneous GI flare and polyarticular large-joint synovitis after a missed biologic dose, with a soft abdomen and no single septic-looking joint, indicates an enteropathic flare — but fluctuant effusions in an immunosuppressed patient plus dehydration mandate urgent in-person assessment, joint aspiration and bloods rather than home steroids.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent in-person joint and abdominal examination
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To assess for a septic joint and a surgical abdomen — neither can be evaluated via chat.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Joint aspiration and synovial fluid microscopy/culture/crystals
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Definitive test to exclude septic arthritis and gout in a fluctuant, immunosuppressed joint.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Bloods (FBC, CRP, U&E, blood cultures if febrile) and IV rehydration
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Assess inflammation/infection, dehydration and renal function; rehydrate.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Empirical home IV steroids via telehealth
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Systemic immunosuppression before excluding sepsis, given remotely, is unsafe.
